subject: English Language Arts
topic: Preparing for a short seminar discussion

teacher_guide.big_idea: Students need a visible structure for taking a position, selecting evidence, and preparing a question before a short discussion.
learning_goals: I can prepare a clear position for discussion.
I can collect evidence before I speak.
I can bring one question into the discussion.

timing.activity: Open the seminar question and model position-building.
Students complete the discussion prep sheet.

teacher_notes: Keep the prep sheet evidence-focused rather than debate-theater focused.
Push students to bring at least one specific piece of evidence into the discussion.

content.subtitle: Prepare your position, evidence, and one real question before the discussion begins.

content.scenario: Should communities put more funding into public libraries or into youth recreation spaces?
prompts: What question do you still need to ask?

discussion_prep_sheet.title: Discussion Prep Sheet
discussion_prep_sheet.discussion_prompt: Should communities put more funding into public libraries or into youth recreation spaces?
success_criteria: I brought one real question into the discussion.

exit_ticket.title: Exit Ticket
exit_ticket.prompt: What helped you feel more ready for the discussion today?
success_criteria: I named one useful preparation move.
